text: Amazon SDE-1 technical interview had 3 rounds. First round focused on LRU Cache implementation using HashMap and DoublyLinkedList with O(1) get and put. Second round was binary tree zigzag traversal. Third was system design — design a URL shortener.

Amazon HR round was 45 minutes. All questions were based on Leadership Principles. Tell me about a time you failed. Why Amazon. Where do you see yourself in 5 years. Prepare STAR stories for each principle.
JP Morgan coding test on HackerRank — 2 hours, 3 problems. First was maximum subarray sum using Kadane algorithm. Second was bank transaction system with timestamps. Third was hard graph problem.
JP Morgan technical interview focused on Java concurrency. Questions on HashMap vs ConcurrentHashMap, thread safety, implement thread-safe LRU Cache using ReentrantReadWriteLock.
TCS NQT aptitude test has three sections — verbal 20 questions, reasoning 20 questions, numerical 20 questions. Negative marking 0.33 per wrong answer. Focus on speed and accuracy.
Infosys InfyTQ coding round on HackerEarth. Two coding problems — longest palindromic substring using DP and minimum spanning tree using Kruskal algorithm. Plus 10 MCQs on data structures.
Microsoft system design round. Design a collaborative document editor like Google Docs. Discuss operational transformation vs CRDT for conflict resolution. Scale to 10 million concurrent users.
Wipro HR interview was very straightforward — 30 minutes video call. Tell me about yourself, why Wipro, are you willing to relocate, strengths and weaknesses, where do you see yourself in 5 years.
Google technical interview — find all bridges in undirected graph using Tarjan algorithm. Also median of data stream using two heaps approach. Speed and code cleanliness both evaluated.
Flipkart technical round — implement hit counter returning count of hits in past 5 minutes using sliding window deque. Design Flipkart product search with Elasticsearch and Redis cache.
Cognizant technical interview focused on OOPs concepts — polymorphism abstraction encapsulation inheritance with real examples. Write program to reverse a linked list iteratively and recursively.
Accenture aptitude and coding test — quantitative aptitude basic maths, verbal reasoning, coding section had simple array manipulation and string problems. Easy difficulty overall.
Oracle technical interview — SQL queries with joins subqueries window functions. Also Java streams and lambda expressions. Database normalization up to 3NF and ACID properties.
Deloitte managerial round — discuss your final year project in detail. How would you handle a situation where your team member is not meeting deadlines. Leadership and conflict resolution questions.
HCL coding round — two easy problems on arrays and strings. First was remove duplicates from sorted array. Second was check if two strings are anagrams. 90 minutes for both problems.
Uber system design interview — design ride sharing system. Cover geolocation, surge pricing algorithm, matching driver to rider, real-time tracking with websockets, database sharding.
Zoho technical interview — pure coding questions on recursion, backtracking, and dynamic programming. No system design. Questions were medium to hard level. Focus on optimal solutions.
Capgemini aptitude test — pseudocode questions where you read code and predict output, game-based assessments, behavioural questions, and essay writing. Different from standard aptitude.
Razorpay backend technical interview — design and implement rate limiter from scratch. Also questions on Redis internals, consistent hashing, and database transactions with isolation levels.
Swiggy system design — design food delivery system like Swiggy. Cover order management, restaurant onboarding, real-time driver tracking, surge pricing, notification service.
Paytm coding round — implement a stack that supports push pop and getMin in O(1) time. Then LRU Cache with get and put in O(1). Both classical data structure problems.
IBM HR round — standard questions about career goals, team work experience, handling failure, why IBM, expected salary. Friendly interviewer. No technical questions in this round.
Samsung R&D coding test — C and C++ focused problems. Memory management, pointers, dynamic arrays. Also embedded systems basics like interrupts and real-time operating systems concepts.
Adobe technical interview — image processing algorithm questions, matrix rotation, convolution operations. Also design Photoshop undo redo feature using command pattern and observer pattern.
Groww backend coding interview — two LeetCode hard problems. First was merge k sorted linked lists using min heap. Second was serialize and deserialize binary tree. Very fast paced.
Juspay functional programming round — Haskell and Scala concepts, pure functions, monads, immutability. Also system design for payment gateway with idempotency and exactly once delivery.
L&T Infotech group discussion round — topic was artificial intelligence in healthcare. 8 candidates discussed for 20 minutes. Evaluate by communication skills, content knowledge, leadership.
Mindtree technical and aptitude combined round. First section was 20 aptitude questions — pipes cisterns time distance. Second section was 2 coding problems on strings and arrays. Easy level.
Goldman Sachs technical interview — quantitative finance questions combined with coding. Implement options pricing model, explain time value of money, also binary search tree operations.
Atlassian system design — design Jira project management system. User stories, sprints, kanban boards, notifications, integrations with GitHub. Scale to 100000 projects.
PhonePe technical interview — implement consistent hashing with virtual nodes for distributed cache. Also questions on Kafka message queues, consumer groups, and exactly once semantics.
Cerner healthcare IT technical interview — HL7 FHIR API standards, EHR system design, patient data security and HIPAA compliance, database design for medical records.
Infosys managerial discussion — why do you want to join Infosys, how do you handle team conflicts, describe a project where you showed leadership, your 5-year plan.
Wipro elite coding assessment — 3 problems in 3 hours. Dynamic programming on matrix, graph BFS DFS problems, and a string manipulation problem. Medium difficulty overall.
Postman technical interview — API design best practices, REST vs GraphQL tradeoffs, design a rate limiter for API gateway, implement circuit breaker pattern in distributed system.
Schlumberger data structures focused round — implement trie for autocomplete, segment tree for range queries, and disjoint set union for network connectivity problems.
Cisco network engineering technical — OSI model layers, TCP UDP differences, subnetting CIDR notation, BGP routing protocol, and software defined networking concepts.
EPAM technical coding — clean code principles, SOLID design principles with examples, implement dependency injection from scratch, unit testing with mocks and stubs.
Freshworks product engineering round — design and code a simplified version of their CRM notification system. Real-time alerts, email triggers, in-app notifications with delivery guarantees.
Airtel digital technical interview — telecom domain knowledge, design billing system for prepaid postpaid customers, event driven architecture for usage tracking and alerts.
